name: run_shell
description: Execute a whitelisted program (python/python3/node/npm/npx/git read-only) with argv arguments. Does NOT invoke a shell interpreter, so shell metacharacters like `;`, `&&`, `|` are treated as literal arguments. Cannot delete files, cannot make network requests, cannot modify files outside registered sources. Use for running data processing scripts, git status/diff inspections, etc. Always requires user confirmation. Output is capped at 64 KB per stream. Default timeout 30s, max 300s.

program.description: Program to execute. Must be one of: python, python3, node, npm, npx, git. No shell interpreter is invoked.

args.description: Arguments passed directly as argv (no shell interpretation). Defaults to []. For git, first arg must be a read-only subcommand (status, diff, log, show, ls-files, rev-parse, branch, tag, config, remote, describe, blame).

cwd.description: Working directory for the command. Must resolve inside a registered source directory (absolute or source-relative).

timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds. Default 30, min 1, max 300. Values outside this range are clamped.
